assertions.evidence:
| Scenario | Shipping Method | Destination | Order Value | Shipping Cost? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Standard, any destination and order value | Standard | {UK, Ireland, International} | {£20, £75} | £3.99 |

assertions.text: Express shipping is split into at least two cases distinguishing the UK free tier (UK, order £50+, free) from the paid tier. The split is driven by the UK-only free threshold rule.
assertions.evidence: | Express, order at threshold in UK | Express | UK | £50 | £0.00 | and | Express, order at or above threshold outside UK | Express | {Ireland, International} | {£50, £75} | £9.99 | — UK gets free tier, non-UK pays £9.99 at same threshold

assertions.text: Express rows use value sets to consolidate cases with the same outcome — e.g. non-UK destinations at/above threshold in one row (such as {Ireland, Other} or {Ireland, International}), or UK at/above threshold in one row ({£50, £75}). Not enumerated as separate rows per destination or per order value when the result is identical. Any reasonable label for non-UK/Ireland destinations is acceptable (International, Other, Rest of World, etc.).
assertions.evidence: | Express, order at or above threshold outside UK | Express | {Ireland, International} | {£50, £75} | £9.99 | — non-UK destinations consolidated with value sets {Ireland, International} and {£50, £75}

assertions.text: Overnight UK and Ireland are in one row using a value set ({UK, Ireland}) — not separate rows, since both have the same cost and availability.
assertions.evidence: | Overnight, UK or Ireland | Overnight | {UK, Ireland} | {£20, £75} | £14.99 |

assertions.text: Standard shipping destination uses a value set covering all options (e.g. {UK, Ireland, International} or {UK, Ireland, Other}) or is blank/absent — either approach is valid since destination is irrelevant to the cost. Any reasonable label for non-UK/Ireland destinations is acceptable.
assertions.evidence: | Standard, any destination and order value | Standard | {UK, Ireland, International} | {£20, £75} | £3.99 |

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: | Scenario | Shipping Method | Destination | Order Value | Shipping Cost? |
